title: Why this chronicle matters now
summary: The point of the page is not to memorialize a completed academic career. It is to show how Juan Santiago's sustained contributions to microfluidics, diagnostics, and transport phenomena have created lasting impact through research, innovation, mentorship, and institutional leadership.

signals.label: Sustained research excellence
signals.text: Santiago's record shows continuous productivity and impact across nearly three decades — from early NSF CAREER and PECASE awards through recent election to the National Academy of Engineering, with 220+ journal papers, 60 patents, and an h-index of 91.

signals.label: National academy recognition
signals.text: Election to three national academies (NAI 2022, AAAS 2022, NAE 2026) represents the highest peer recognition in American science and engineering, confirming transformative contributions to microfluidics and related fields.

signals.label: Mentorship legacy and institutional leadership
signals.text: As Vice Chair of Stanford's Mechanical Engineering Department and Editor-in-Chief of Cambridge's Flow journal, Santiago shapes both his institution and his field. His 32 former PhD students and postdocs — 24 now faculty at major universities — extend his intellectual legacy across the research community.
